summary: India must actively fund and underwrite infrastructure projects in its neighbouring countries to ensure stable and durable regional ties, especially amidst growing political changes and competition from China’s Belt and Road Initiative (BRI). External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar emphasized that this approach, central to India’s Neighbourhood First policy, aims to create shared interests and act as a stabilizing factor, helping safeguard relationships even if neighbouring governments change. India’s recent push includes major rail projects with Bhutan, financial aid to Sri Lanka, and broader transport corridors like the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor, while China’s investments through BRI continue to expand its influence in South Asia. The minister highlighted the importance of connectivity, strategic infrastructure, and economic engagement to make India the “go-to” partner in the region, while remaining flexible to accommodate other interests within its national advantage.
